document.title: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Essential Principles checklist for the Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1), identifying applicable safety and performance principles and supporting conformity documents.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports medical device regulatory registration by mapping essential safety and performance requirements to risk management, testing, software validation, clinical evaluation, labeling, and other conformity evidence.
keyPoints: EP Checklist control number is A-EPC-HNX.
Product owner is H&abyz Co., Ltd and product name is Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1).

Applicable conformity methods include risk management, safety testing, EMC testing, wireless EMC testing, software development and validation, usability testing, clinical evaluation, labeling, and user manual evidence.
The device emits radiation and includes applicable sections for protection against intended, unintended, and ionising radiation.
Several clauses are marked N/A, including medicinal product substances, sterilisation, measuring function, therapeutic radiology, and active implantable device requirements.

evidence.claim: The checklist states the device is not related with medicinal products or any substances.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not related with medicinal products or any substances
evidence.pageTarget: Medicinal Product Substance in Medical Devices

evidence.claim: The checklist states the device is not intended for sterilisation.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended for sterilisation.

evidence.claim: The checklist states the device has no measuring function.
evidence.evidenceText: N/A N/A No measuring function

evidence.claim: Radiation protection clauses are applicable to the device.
evidence.evidenceText: 12. Protection against radiation 12.1 General Medical devices shall be designed and manufactured and packaged in such a way that exposure of patients, users and other persons to any emitted radiation shall be reduced as far as practicable and appropriate

evidence.claim: Ionising radiation clauses for diagnostic radiology are applicable to the device.
evidence.evidenceText: 12.5.2 Medical devices emitting ionising radiation intended for diagnostic radiology shall be designed and manufactured in such a way as to achieve appropriate image and/or output quality for the intended medical purpose whilst minimising radiation exposure of the patient and user. A Risk Management

evidence.claim: Therapeutic radiology requirements are not applicable because the device is not intended for therapeutic radiology.
evidence.evidenceText: 12.5.3 Medical devices emitting ionising radiation, intended for therapeutic radiology... N/A N/A It is not intended for therapeutic radiology.

evidence.claim: The device depends on an internal power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.4 Medical devices where the safety of the patients depends on an external power supply shall include an alarm system to signal any power failure. N/A N/A The device depends on an internal power supply.

evidence.claim: The device is described as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is not designed for lay persons and is for radiologist use only.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not designed for lay persons, not applicable. Radiologist use only.

evidence.claim: Information supplied by the product owner is supported by label and packaging specification A-LPS-HNX and user manuals A-IFU-HNX and A-IFU-HNX(B).
evidence.evidenceText: Label and packaging specification: A-LPS-HNX User Manual: A-IFU-HNX and A-IFU-HNX(B)
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Labeling and Instructions for Use

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations on human subjects are stated to be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration, but the device is not for clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical investigations on human subjects shall be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration... N/A N/A The device is not for clinical investigation, not applicable with this matter.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is FDA in source metadata, but the provided source text does not identify FDA or another regulatory authority/jurisdiction.
Document revision or version is not present; only the EP Checklist control number is shown.

OCR/text extraction quality issues are present, including replacement characters, broken line wraps, duplicated clause text, and incomplete entries such as a Post-Market Surveillance report field without a report number.
The document date is inferred from the prepared-by line rather than a dedicated document date field.
